chapters.laptop-vs-server: Why rdc lives on your laptop

events.text: Here we go. Paste the install command into your terminal and let it run. The script verifies your platform, downloads the latest stable binary, and drops it into your home directory. No system-wide install, no sudo. Watch it work. The binary is on disk and you can call it from any shell.
events.afterText: Install done. The rdc binary is on your path.
events.prose: Paste the install command into your terminal and run it. The script verifies your platform, downloads the latest stable binary, and drops it into your home directory. No system-wide install, no sudo required. Once it finishes, the rdc binary is on your path and callable from any shell.

events.text: Install done. Let's confirm it. The version flag prints what just got installed, so we know it's on your path.
events.afterText: Version printed. You're good to go.
events.prose: Confirm the install by running rdc with the version flag. It prints the installed version, verifying the binary is on your path.

narrations.text: Alright! Let's get the command-line tool installed on your laptop.
Three steps. Open the install page, pick your operating system, then paste the command into your terminal.
Head to the install page and scroll down to the quick install section. It generates the right one-liner for whatever platform you're on.
The command-line tool lives on your laptop. The server runs a separate component called renet, which the CLI provisions and drives over SSH, so you never need to log in manually.
That's the install done. Next up, we'll set up your SSH key so the CLI can reach your servers.
